# signal CLI 名前空間統一と gate strictness の宣言的管理

## Tasks (0/13 resolved)

### S1 — Domain Foundation: Chain Types, Traits, and Blanket Impl (D7)

> Establish the Rust type foundation in `libs/domain/src/chain.rs`: the four chain value-object types (ChainId, Strictness, GateKind, ChainGateEntry, SignalGateMatrix), the four trait definitions (ChainIdentity, SoTChain, LiveSoTChain, PersistedSoTChain), and the `impl<T: PersistedSoTChain> SoTChain for T` blanket impl.
> Extract the chain ② Red/Yellow gate logic into `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals` — a pure domain function symmetric with the existing chain ① and ③ pure functions.

- [~] **T001**: D7: Add `libs/domain/src/chain.rs` (new file) with: `ChainId` enum (AdrUser/SpecAdr/CatalogSpec/ImplCatalog), `Strictness` enum (Strict/Interim), `GateKind` enum (Commit/Merge), `ChainGateEntry` struct with `commit_gate: Strictness` + `merge_gate: Strictness` + `resolve(GateKind) -> Strictness` method, `SignalGateMatrix` struct with four `ChainGateEntry` fields + `resolve(ChainId, GateKind) -> Strictness` method, and four trait definitions: `ChainIdentity` (associated const ID + GAT Input), `SoTChain: ChainIdentity` (associated fn `check`), `LiveSoTChain: SoTChain` (associated fn `calc_live`), `PersistedSoTChain: ChainIdentity` (associated fns `calc`, `load`, `check_freshness`, `evaluate_gate`, `calc_error`, `stale_error`), plus `impl<T: PersistedSoTChain> SoTChain for T` blanket impl. Re-export types from `libs/domain/src/lib.rs`. Standard derives: Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq for all enums; Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq for structs. No serde on domain types (serde-free constraint). Unit tests: ChainGateEntry::resolve returns correct Strictness for each GateKind variant; SignalGateMatrix::resolve returns correct cell for each (ChainId, GateKind) pair. Estimated diff: ~200 lines.
- [ ] **T002**: D2 / CN-05: Add `domain::chain::check_catalogue_spec_signals(doc: &CatalogueSpecSignalsDocument, strict: bool) -> VerifyOutcome` pure domain free function in `libs/domain/src/chain.rs` (or a sub-module `libs/domain/src/chain/check.rs` re-exported from `chain`). This function extracts the Red/Yellow gate logic currently duplicated in `libs/usecase/src/merge_gate/chain2_gate.rs` (inline) and `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/catalogue_spec_signals.rs` (inline) into a single domain-layer pure function, symmetric with the existing `check_spec_doc_signals` (chain ①) and `check_type_signals` (chain ③). Rules: Red signals always `Finding::error` regardless of `strict`; Yellow signals → `Finding::error` when `strict=true`, `Finding::warning` when `strict=false`. Export from `libs/domain/src/lib.rs`. Unit tests: all-blue doc passes; red signal errors regardless of strict; yellow strict=true errors; yellow strict=false warns. Estimated diff: ~100 lines.

### S2 — Infrastructure Config Loader and Harness Config Files (D3 / D4 / D6)

> Add the `signal_gates_config` infrastructure module: DTO structs, `SignalGatesConfigError`, and `load_signal_gates_config` — the single function that reads, validates, and converts `.harness/config/signal-gates.json` to `domain::SignalGateMatrix` with hard errors on any problem.
> Commit the recommended default `.harness/config/signal-gates.json` and the ADR-strict sample variant. Update `responsibility-boundary.md`.

- [ ] **T003**: D3 / D4: Add `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/signal_gates_config.rs` (new module) with: `StrictnessDto` serde boundary enum mirroring `domain::Strictness` (loader validation maps unknown strictness strings to `InvalidValue`; malformed JSON remains `ParseFailed`); `GateRowDto` serde struct with four `StrictnessDto` fields (adr_user, spec_adr, catalog_spec, impl_catalog); `SignalGatesConfig` serde struct with `schema_version: u32` + `commit_gate: GateRowDto` + `merge_gate: GateRowDto` (`#[serde(rename = "$schema_version")]` for the dollar-prefixed key); `SignalGatesConfigError` enum (FileNotFound, ParseFailed, SchemaVersionUnknown, MissingKey, InvalidValue) with Display + Error impls; `load_signal_gates_config(config_path: PathBuf) -> Result<domain::SignalGateMatrix, SignalGatesConfigError>` free function that reads, parses, validates `$schema_version == 1`, checks all required keys, and converts to `domain::SignalGateMatrix` — no implicit fallback on any error. `From<StrictnessDto> for domain::Strictness` impl. Re-export from `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/mod.rs`. Unit tests: valid config round-trips correctly; file-not-found returns FileNotFound; malformed JSON returns ParseFailed; unknown schema_version returns SchemaVersionUnknown; missing key returns MissingKey; invalid strictness string returns InvalidValue. Estimated diff: ~220 lines.
- [ ] **T004**: D6: (1) Create `.harness/config/signal-gates.json` with the recommended default (commit_gate: adr_user=interim, spec_adr=strict, catalog_spec=strict, impl_catalog=interim; merge_gate: all strict) and `$schema_version: 1`. (2) Create `.harness/config/samples/signal-gates.adr-strict.json` with commit_gate.adr_user=strict variant for users who want adr_user strict at commit time. (3) Update `knowledge/conventions/responsibility-boundary.md` to add `.harness/config/signal-gates.json` to the template-user-editable list, noting the distinction between commit_gate.impl_catalog=interim (TDDD structural necessity) vs commit_gate.adr_user=interim (SoTOHE workflow choice). Estimated diff: ~70 lines.

### S3 — Usecase Chain Structs and Trait Impls (D7)

> Implement the four chain structs in `libs/usecase/src/chain/`: AdrUserChain (LiveSoTChain, no persistence), SpecAdrChain, CatalogSpecChain, ImplCatalogChain (all PersistedSoTChain, gaining SoTChain via blanket impl). Each struct wires its evaluate_gate to the appropriate domain pure function and its check_freshness to the appropriate hash comparison mechanism.

- [ ] **T005**: D7: Add `libs/usecase/src/chain/` directory with four sub-modules: `adr_user.rs` (AdrUserChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + SoTChain + LiveSoTChain — live scan of knowledge/adr/ frontmatter, no persistence file); `spec_adr.rs` (SpecAdrChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain — `evaluate_gate` delegates to `domain::check_spec_doc_signals`, `check_freshness` implements self-consistency check comparing `doc.evaluate_signals()` vs `doc.signals()`); `catalog_spec.rs` (CatalogSpecChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain — `evaluate_gate` delegates to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals` (T002), `check_freshness` compares entry_hash from signals doc with current catalogue bytes SHA-256); `impl_catalog.rs` (ImplCatalogChain unit struct implementing ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain — `evaluate_gate` delegates to `domain::check_type_signals`, `check_freshness` compares declaration_hash from type-signals doc with current catalogue bytes SHA-256). Add `mod.rs` to re-export all four. All four get SoTChain via blanket impl from T001 (chains ①②③) or direct impl (chain ⓪). Unit/static trait-bound tests: assert AdrUserChain satisfies ChainIdentity + SoTChain + LiveSoTChain, assert SpecAdrChain/CatalogSpecChain/ImplCatalogChain satisfy ChainIdentity + PersistedSoTChain and are accepted by SoTChain-bound helper functions through the blanket impl, and cover freshness/evaluate_gate delegation happy/error paths with mocked persisted documents. Depends on T001 and T002. Estimated diff: ~350 lines.

### S4 — Infrastructure Verify Rewiring: strict params, Stage1/Stage2 split, domain delegation (D2)

> Add `strict: bool` to `execute_verify_adr_signals`. Add `read_adr_verify_report` to `TrackBlobReader`. Wire chain ⓪ into `check_strict_merge_gate`.
> Split `verify_from_spec_json` into Stage1 (check-spec-adr with self-consistency freshness check) and separate Stage2 (check-impl-catalog with independent strict).
> Replace inline Red/Yellow logic in `execute_catalogue_spec_signals*` and `chain2_gate.rs` with calls to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals`.

- [ ] **T006**: D2 / D5: (1) Modify `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/adr_signals.rs::execute_verify_adr_signals` to accept `strict: bool` parameter — when `strict=true`, `ReviewFindingRef` (Yellow) promotes to `Finding::error`; Red (`NoGrounds`) always errors; `Grandfathered` always skip. (2) Add `read_adr_verify_report(&self, branch: String) -> BlobFetchResult<domain::AdrVerifyReport>` method to the `TrackBlobReader` trait in `libs/usecase/src/merge_gate.rs`, with a corresponding adapter impl that scans ADR frontmatter for the target branch and returns the domain report without exposing a filesystem path to the usecase layer. (3) Modify the merge-gate caller at the CLI/infrastructure boundary to load `SignalGateMatrix` via `load_signal_gates_config` and pass the validated domain matrix into `usecase::merge_gate::check_strict_merge_gate`; the usecase function itself must not depend on infrastructure. (4) Modify `check_strict_merge_gate` to accept that `domain::SignalGateMatrix`, pass its existing `branch` argument to `reader.read_adr_verify_report(branch)`, resolve `GateKind::Merge` strictness for all four cells (`adr_user`, `spec_adr`, `catalog_spec`, `impl_catalog`), use the resolved strict bools for the existing chain ①②③ evaluations instead of hardcoded `strict=true`, and add chain ⓪ evaluation via `AdrUserChain::check` (or equivalent report-to-outcome path) with the resolved `merge_gate.adr_user` strictness. Depends on T001, T003, T005. Estimated diff: ~200 lines.
- [ ] **T007**: D2 / IN-03 / CN-04: Modify `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/spec_states.rs::verify_from_spec_json` to split Stage1 and Stage2. Stage1 (chain ① `check-spec-adr`): keep `check_spec_doc_signals(&spec_doc, strict)` — add the new self-consistency freshness check: compute `spec_doc.evaluate_signals()` and compare with stored `spec_doc.signals()`; if they differ, return stale error with a message prompting `signal calc-spec-adr`. Stage2 (chain ③ `check-impl-catalog`): extract into a new separate public function (e.g., `verify_type_signals_from_spec_json` or similar) callable independently by the new `signal check-impl-catalog` CLI command, accepting its own `strict: bool`. The existing `verify_from_spec_json` signature is modified per the infrastructure-types.json catalogue (Stage1 only). Ensure backward-compat compile: any internal callers of the old combined function are updated in this same task. Estimated diff: ~200 lines.
- [ ] **T008**: D2 / CN-05 / AC-03: Modify `libs/infrastructure/src/verify/catalogue_spec_signals.rs`: (1) `execute_catalogue_spec_signals` — replace the inline Red/Yellow evaluation logic with a call to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals(&doc, strict)` from T002. (2) `execute_catalogue_spec_signals_check` — same delegation. Also remove the corresponding inline logic from `libs/usecase/src/merge_gate/chain2_gate.rs::check_chain2_for_layer` and replace with a call to `domain::check_catalogue_spec_signals`. ### S5 — CLI signal Namespace: 8 Commands + Aggregate check (D1 / D5)

> Add `apps/cli/src/commands/signal/` with the 8 orthogonal commands (`calc-*` / `check-*` × 4 chains) and the aggregate `signal check --gate commit|merge`. CLI surface uses `catalog` spelling. Each `check-*` resolves strictness from `--strict` or from `load_signal_gates_config` when `--gate` is specified.

- [ ] **T009**: D1 / D5 / AC-01 / AC-11: Add `apps/cli/src/commands/signal/` module with 8 individual commands and 1 aggregate: `calc_adr_user.rs` (delegates to AdrUserChain::calc_live via LiveSoTChain, no-persist, live display); `check_adr_user.rs` (delegates to AdrUserChain::check via SoTChain, resolves strictness from --strict flag or --gate + load_signal_gates_config); `calc_spec_adr.rs` (delegates to SpecAdrChain::calc via PersistedSoTChain, persists signals to spec.json); `check_spec_adr.rs` (delegates to SpecAdrChain::check via SoTChain with freshness, resolves strictness); `calc_catalog_spec.rs` (delegates to CatalogSpecChain::calc); `check_catalog_spec.rs` (delegates to CatalogSpecChain::check with entry_hash freshness); `calc_impl_catalog.rs` (delegates to ImplCatalogChain::calc); `check_impl_catalog.rs` (delegates to ImplCatalogChain::check with declaration_hash freshness); `mod.rs` with SignalCommand enum routing all 8 sub-commands plus aggregate `check --gate commit|merge`. Add `signal` to the top-level sotp command enum. UI uses `catalog` spelling (not `catalogue`). Each `check-*` accepts `--strict` override and `--gate commit|merge` for config-driven resolution. The aggregate `signal check --gate` runs all 4 chains in order and aggregates findings. Tests: parser/dispatch tests for all 8 commands and aggregate `signal check --gate`, strict-vs-gate resolution tests (including invalid flag combinations if mutually exclusive), config-driven strictness cell selection for commit/merge, aggregate all-four-chain execution with mocked outcomes, and CLI surface assertions that command names use `catalog` spelling and no old command aliases remain. Depends on T001, T003, T005, T006, T007, T008. Estimated diff: ~420 lines.

### S7 — Config Validation Tests (D4)

> Comprehensive unit tests for `load_signal_gates_config` covering all hard-error branches: file not found, parse failure, schema version unknown, missing key, invalid value. Integration test that the committed default config parses to the expected SignalGateMatrix.

- [ ] **T013**: D4 / AC-05: Add comprehensive unit tests for `load_signal_gates_config` covering all hard-error scenarios: (1) file not found → `SignalGatesConfigError::FileNotFound`; (2) malformed JSON → `ParseFailed`; (3) `$schema_version` = 0 or 99 → `SchemaVersionUnknown`; (4) missing `commit_gate` object → `MissingKey`; (5) missing individual chain×gate cell → `MissingKey`; (6) invalid strictness string (e.g. `"permissive"`) → `InvalidValue`; (7) valid config with recommended defaults → Ok with expected `SignalGateMatrix`; (8) valid config with all-strict → Ok. Also add integration-level test verifying that the committed `.harness/config/signal-gates.json` (T004) parses successfully. Add unit tests for `SignalGatesConfigError::Display` variants (actionable messages). Depends on T003, T004. Estimated diff: ~200 lines.
